action.reasoning: Observation: The evaluation succeeded with an EER of 12.59% and accuracy of 87.46%. This is a strong baseline for just 2 epochs of training with WavLM-large. Analysis: The model is learning well, and we have plenty of time budget (7.7 hours left). Iteration 6 took ~9.5 minutes for 2 epochs, so 1 epoch takes ~4.75 minutes. We can scale up the training to 15 epochs (~70 minutes) to allow the model to converge. To prevent overfitting and improve generalization to the out-of-domain test set (which contains unseen codecs and attacks), I will add basic data augmentations (random noise, volume scaling, and cutout) to the training dataset. I will also add a CosineAnnealingLR scheduler and save the best model based on validation EER. Decision: Update `dataset.py` with augmentations, update `train.py` to train for 15 epochs with a scheduler and save `best.pt`, run the training, and submit the evaluation using the best checkpoint.
